seoTitle: Bussmann Fuse Products | Low Voltage, Semiconductor, EV Fuses Distributor
seoDescription: Browse Bussmann fuse products including low voltage fuses, semiconductor fuses, and EV fuses. Authorized distributor with technical support and selection guide.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Class J and Class RK fuses?
faqs.answer: Class J and Class RK fuses have different characteristics: Class J fuses offer space-efficient design at 600V rating with excellent current limitation. Class RK fuses provide dual-element time-delay design for motor circuits with superior motor starting protection. Class RK1 offers highest current limitation, Class RK5 offers lower cost.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Class J for space-constrained applications. Use Class RK for motor circuits requiring time-delay.

faqs.question: How do I select the right fuse for motor protection?
faqs.answer: Motor fuse selection: 1) Use Class RK or J fuses with adequate time-delay for motor starting; 2) Size at 125-175% of motor full-load current; 3) Consider service factor and starting conditions; 4) Coordinate with motor starter overloads; 5) Verify breaking capacity exceeds available fault current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Size at 125-175% of FLA. Use time-delay fuses for motor circuits.

faqs.question: What is gPV certification for solar fuses?
faqs.answer: gPV (general Purpose Photovoltaic) certification per IEC 60269-7 is required for solar fuses. It indicates the fuse is tested for DC operation up to 1500V and can safely interrupt low overcurrents typical of PV faults. Standard fuses are not suitable for PV applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use gPV-certified fuses for solar/photovoltaic applications.

faqs.question: What breaking capacity do I need?
faqs.answer: Breaking capacity must exceed maximum prospective fault current. Typical requirements: 100kA for standard distribution, 200kA for large industrial systems. Calculate based on transformer kVA and impedance. Add 25% margin. Bussmann offers fuses with breaking capacity up to 300kA.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate fault current and select fuse with adequate margin.

faqs.question: What is the difference between fast-acting and time-delay fuses?
faqs.answer: Fast-acting fuses open quickly on overcurrent, ideal for protecting sensitive electronics and preventing wire damage. Time-delay fuses can withstand temporary overloads (motor starting, inrush) without opening. Use fast-acting for general circuits, time-delay for motor circuits with high starting currents.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use fast-acting for general circuits. Use time-delay for motors and circuits with inrush.

categories.name: Low Voltage Fuses
categories.description: Bussmann low voltage fuses provide reliable circuit protection for industrial, commercial, and residential applications. Available in various classes including Class J, RK, T, CC, and G with ratings from fractions of an ampere to thousands of amperes.
categories.longDescription: Bussmann low voltage fuses offer comprehensive protection solutions for electrical distribution systems. As an authorized Bussmann distributor, LiTong provides access to the complete range of Class J, Class RK, Class T, and specialty fuses. Class J fuses feature space-efficient designs with 600V ratings and excellent current limitation for branch circuit protection. Class RK fuses provide dual-element time-delay characteristics essential for motor circuit protection. Our selection guide helps you choose the right fuse based on voltage, current, breaking capacity, and application requirements. All fuses meet UL, CSA, and IEC standards for global applications.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Low Voltage Fuses
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting low voltage fuses based on voltage, current, breaking capacity, and application requirements.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Class J and Class RK fuses?
faqs.answer: Class J fuses are space-efficient with 600V rating and excellent current limitation. Class RK fuses offer dual-element time-delay design for motor circuits with superior starting protection. Class RK1 provides highest current limitation, Class RK5 offers lower cost.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Class J for space savings. Use Class RK for motor circuits requiring time-delay.

faqs.question: What breaking capacity do I need for industrial panels?
faqs.answer: Breaking capacity must exceed maximum prospective fault current. Calculate based on transformer kVA and impedance. Most industrial facilities need 100kA minimum, with larger systems requiring 200kA or higher. Bussmann offers fuses with breaking capacity up to 300kA.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate fault current and add 25% margin when selecting breaking capacity.

faqs.question: Can Class J fuses replace Class RK fuses?
faqs.answer: Class J fuses can replace Class RK fuses only if the voltage rating is adequate and the fuse block/holder is compatible. Class J fuses are smaller and require Class J mounting. Never substitute a higher ampere rating fuse.
faqs.decisionGuide: Class J can replace RK with compatible holder. Consult electrician for proper replacement.

faqs.question: What fuse class should I use for motor protection?
faqs.answer: Use Class RK fuses for motor circuits. The dual-element time-delay design handles motor starting currents (500-600% of rated current) without nuisance opening. Size at 125-175% of motor full-load current per NEC requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select Class RK for motor circuits. Size at 125-175% of FLA.

faqs.question: How do I ensure selective coordination?
faqs.answer: Selective coordination ensures only the fuse closest to the fault operates. Maintain 2:1 time-current ratio between upstream and downstream devices. Use manufacturer coordination tables for verification.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use coordination tables and verify time-current separation.

products.name: 30A 600V Class J Fast-Acting Fuse
products.shortDescription: 30A 600V Class J fast-acting fuse with 200kA breaking capacity for branch circuit protection in industrial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The JKS-30 is a high-performance Class J fuse designed for branch circuit protection in industrial applications.
With 600V rating and 30A current capacity, this fuse provides reliable protection for control circuits and feeders.
The 200kA breaking capacity ensures safe interruption of severe fault currents.

faeReview.author: Robert Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Systems
faeReview.content: Class J fuses have been my go-to recommendation for industrial branch circuits for over 20 years. The JKS-30 offers an excellent balance of performance, size, and cost. The 200kA breaking capacity handles even the largest industrial fault currents, providing safety margin. I particularly appreciate the compact size - Class J fuses are significantly smaller than equivalent Class RK fuses, which is valuable in space-constrained panels. The fast-acting characteristic provides excellent protection for control circuits and minimizes wire damage during faults. Bussmann quality is consistently high, with reliable performance across temperature ranges. I recommend this fuse for any 30A branch circuit application.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable branch circuit protection with excellent breaking capacity and compact size

alternativeParts.reason: Higher breaking capacity for large fault currents
alternativeParts.useCase: Systems with very high available fault current

companionParts.description: 600V Class J fuse block for panel mounting

faqs.question: Can I replace a Class RK fuse with a Class J fuse?
faqs.answer: Class J fuses can replace Class RK fuses only if the voltage rating is adequate and the physical space allows. Class J fuses are smaller but require Class J fuse blocks/holders. Never install a higher rated fuse than specified. Consult an electrician for proper replacement.
faqs.decisionGuide: Class J can replace RK if voltage adequate and holder compatible. Consult electrician.

faqs.question: What is the maximum fault current this fuse can interrupt?
faqs.answer: The JKS-30 has a breaking capacity of 200kA AC. This means it can safely interrupt fault currents up to 200,000 amperes. For applications with higher available fault currents, consider the LPJ-30SP with 300kA breaking capacity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify your system's available fault current. Use LPJ series for higher fault currents.

faqs.question: Is this fuse suitable for motor circuits?
faqs.answer: The JKS-30 is a fast-acting fuse designed for branch circuits, not motor circuits. For motor protection, use Class RK time-delay fuses that can handle motor starting currents without nuisance opening.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Class RK fuses for motor circuits. Use JKS for branch circuits only.

faqs.question: What standards does this fuse meet?
faqs.answer: The JKS-30 meets UL 248-8 and CSA C22.2 standards. It is UL Listed and CSA Certified for North American applications. The fuse also meets IEC 60269 standards for international use.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify required certifications for your application. Contact us for certification documentation.

faqs.question: What is the physical size of this fuse?
faqs.answer: Class J fuses have a compact design. The JKS-30 measures approximately 2.5 inches in length with a diameter of about 0.8 inches. This compact size allows more fuses in limited panel space compared to Class RK fuses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify physical dimensions fit your fuse block and panel space requirements.

products.name: 30A 250V Class RK5 Time-Delay Fuse
products.shortDescription: 30A 250V Class RK5 dual-element time-delay fuse specifically designed for motor circuit protection with excellent starting characteristics.
descriptionParagraphs: The FRN-R-30 is a dual-element time-delay fuse designed specifically for motor circuit protection.
With 250V rating and 30A current capacity, this fuse handles motor starting currents without nuisance opening.
The RK5 design provides excellent protection with superior time-delay characteristics.

faeReview.author: Michael Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Motor Control
faeReview.content: The FRN-R series has been the standard for motor protection for decades. The dual-element design handles motor starting currents beautifully - typically 500% of rated current for 10-20 seconds without opening. This is essential for across-the-line motor starting. For motor circuits, the combination of time-delay fuse and motor starter overloads provides comprehensive protection. The RK5 class offers an economical choice while still providing adequate current limitation. I've seen these fuses reliably protect motors for many years in harsh industrial environments. I recommend sizing at 125-150% of motor FLA for optimal protection.
faeReview.highlight: Economical and reliable motor protection with excellent time-delay characteristics

alternativeParts.useCase: Higher horsepower motors up to 10HP at 230V

companionParts.description: Across-the-line motor starter with overload protection
Fused disconnect switch for motor circuits

faqs.question: What size fuse should I use for motor protection?
faqs.answer: Motor fuse sizing: Size at 125-175% of motor full-load current depending on NEC code and motor type. For example: 10HP motor at 230V = 28A FLA, use 35A or 40A fuse (125-140%). Always verify with motor nameplate and NEC requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Size at 125-175% of FLA. Consult NEC for specific requirements.

faqs.question: How does the time-delay feature work?
faqs.answer: The dual-element design has a heat sink element that absorbs energy during temporary overloads like motor starting. This allows 500-600% of rated current for 10-20 seconds without opening. The short-circuit element opens quickly on severe faults.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use time-delay fuses for any circuit with motor starting or transformer inrush.

faqs.question: What is the difference between RK1 and RK5?
faqs.answer: RK1 provides the highest current limitation with superior arc flash reduction. RK5 offers economical protection with good time-delay but lower current limitation. For critical applications, RK1 is preferred.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use RK1 for superior protection. Use RK5 for economical general motor protection.

faqs.question: Can I use this fuse for 480V systems?
faqs.answer: No, the FRN-R-30 is rated for 250V AC. For 480V systems, use FRN-R-30 fuses with 600V rating (FRS-R series) or Class J fuses rated for 600V.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify voltage rating matches your system. Use 600V rated fuses for 480V systems.

faqs.question: What is the typical lifespan of this fuse?
faqs.answer: Under normal conditions, fuses last many years. However, each overload event causes minor aging. Motors with frequent starting may require more frequent fuse inspection. Replace fuses that show signs of heating or damage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Inspect fuses annually. Replace if showing signs of heating or after severe fault events.

categories.name: Semiconductor Fuses
categories.description: Bussmann semiconductor fuses provide ultra-fast protection for power electronics including IGBTs, SCRs, and diodes. These high-speed fuses feature low I2t values, current limitation, and high breaking capacity for protecting expensive semiconductor devices.
categories.longDescription: Bussmann semiconductor fuses are specifically designed to protect power semiconductors including IGBTs, SCRs, and diodes in power conversion applications. As an authorized Bussmann distributor, LiTong provides high-speed fuses with ultra-low I2t values essential for semiconductor protection. The 170M series and FWP series offer ratings from 500V to 1500V with breaking capacities up to 200kA. Key features include bolt-on mounting for secure connections, current-limiting designs to reduce fault energy, and fast clearing times under 1ms. Our selection guide helps you achieve proper I2t coordination between fuses and semiconductors.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Semiconductor Fuses
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting high-speed fuses for semiconductor protection based on I2t coordination and voltage requirements.

faqs.question: What is I2t coordination for semiconductor protection?
faqs.answer: I2t coordination ensures fuse clearing I2t is less than semiconductor withstand I2t. Calculate fuse clearing I2t from curves at fault current. Semiconductor datasheet specifies withstand I2t. Select fuse with 20% margin below semiconductor rating.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure fuse I2t < 0.8 x semiconductor withstand I2t.

faqs.question: Why do semiconductors need special fuses?
faqs.answer: Semiconductors are damaged by overcurrent much faster than wires or transformers. Standard fuses open too slowly to protect IGBTs and diodes. Semiconductor fuses clear in under 1ms to prevent thermal damage to expensive devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use high-speed semiconductor fuses for IGBT and diode protection.

faqs.question: How do I calculate I2t values?
faqs.answer: I2t is the integral of current squared over time. Fuse datasheets provide clearing I2t at various voltages. Semiconductor datasheets specify maximum withstand I2t. Select fuse with clearing I2t less than 80% of semiconductor rating.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use manufacturer coordination tools or consult our FAE for I2t calculations.

faqs.question: What voltage rating do I need for 690V systems?
faqs.answer: For 690V AC systems, select 700V or higher rated fuses. For 690V DC systems, consider 1000V rated fuses to account for DC reversal and transients. Always include voltage margin.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select fuse voltage rating at least 20% above maximum circuit voltage.

faqs.question: Can I use semiconductor fuses for general protection?
faqs.answer: While possible, semiconductor fuses are optimized for fast clearing and may nuisance open on transformer inrush or motor starting. For general circuits, use Class J or RK fuses which are more economical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use semiconductor fuses only for semiconductor protection. Use standard fuses for general circuits.

products.name: 160A 700V High-Speed Semiconductor Fuse
products.shortDescription: 160A 700V high-speed fuse with ultra-low I2t for IGBT protection in power electronics and motor drive applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The 170M5816 is a high-performance semiconductor fuse designed for IGBT module protection.
With 700V rating and 160A current capacity, this fuse provides reliable protection for medium-power converters.
The ultra-low I2t minimizes thermal stress on protected semiconductor devices.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The 170M series has been the industry standard for semiconductor protection for decades. The 170M5816 is ideal for protecting 160A-200A IGBT modules in 690V motor drive applications. The ultra-low I2t is critical for protecting modern IGBTs - these devices are expensive and sensitive to overcurrent. Bussmann's consistent quality ensures reliable protection across temperature ranges and long-term operation. The breaking capacity of 200kA provides safety margin for even large industrial systems. I've specified this series for hundreds of projects with zero fuse-related failures. I strongly recommend proper I2t coordination analysis for every application.
faeReview.highlight: Industry-standard semiconductor protection with proven reliability

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current for larger IGBT modules
alternativeParts.useCase: Higher power converters up to 200A IGBTs

alternativeParts.reason: Lower current for smaller IGBT modules

companionParts.description: Compatible IGBT module for this fuse rating

faqs.question: What is the opening time of this fuse?
faqs.answer: The 170M5816 opens in less than 1ms at 10x rated current (1600A). At lower overcurrents (2-5x rating), opening time is longer but still fast enough to protect semiconductors. Time-current curves in the datasheet show precise opening times at various currents.
faqs.decisionGuide: Check time-current curves for specific opening times.

faqs.question: What is the I2t value of this fuse?
faqs.answer: The clearing I2t varies with voltage and fault current. At 700V and 10kA fault current, typical clearing I2t is approximately 80,000 A2s. Consult the datasheet for specific values at your operating conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify I2t coordination with your semiconductor datasheet.

faqs.question: Can this fuse be used for DC applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 170M5816 is rated for DC applications up to 700VDC. For DC use, ensure proper fuse holder selection and verify breaking capacity at DC voltage. DC arc interruption requires proper fuse design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify DC ratings in datasheet. Use appropriate DC-rated fuse holders.

faqs.question: What size IGBT module can this fuse protect?
faqs.answer: The 170M5816 is suitable for protecting IGBT modules rated 150A-200A at 600V-690V. Verify the IGBT datasheet withstand I2t and ensure fuse clearing I2t is less than 80% of IGBT rating.
faqs.decisionGuide: Perform I2t coordination analysis with your specific IGBT module.

faqs.question: What is the recommended torque for mounting?
faqs.answer: Bolt-on semiconductor fuses typically require 15-20 Nm mounting torque. Use calibrated torque wrench and verify connection resistance after installation. Proper torque ensures good electrical contact and thermal performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use specified torque values. Verify connections with micro-ohmmeter.

categories.name: EV Fuses
categories.description: Bussmann EV fuses provide specialized high-voltage DC protection for electric vehicles, charging infrastructure, and battery systems. These fuses are designed for 500V-1000VDC applications with breaking capacities up to 100kA and AEC-Q200 qualification.
categories.longDescription: Bussmann EV fuses are engineered for the demanding requirements of electric vehicle applications. As an authorized Bussmann distributor, LiTong provides AEC-Q200 qualified fuses for battery pack protection, motor controllers, onboard chargers, and DC/DC converters. The EV series offers ratings from 500V to 1000V DC with breaking capacities up to 100kA. Key features include compact designs for battery pack integration, bolt-on mounting for high-current applications, and automotive-grade reliability. Our selection guide helps you choose fuses with proper voltage margin, breaking capacity, and coordination with battery management systems.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select EV Fuses
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting fuses for electric vehicle applications based on voltage, current, and breaking capacity.

faqs.question: What voltage is needed for EV battery fuses?
faqs.answer: EV battery fuse voltage depends on system voltage: 500V systems use 500V fuses, 800V systems use 1000V fuses. Always select rating 20% above nominal DC voltage. For 400V systems, use 500V fuses. For 800V systems, use 1000V fuses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select voltage 20% above nominal system voltage.

faqs.question: What is AEC-Q200 qualification?
faqs.answer: AEC-Q200 is the automotive standard for passive component reliability. It includes tests for temperature cycling, vibration, humidity, and thermal shock. EV fuses must meet AEC-Q200 for automotive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always verify AEC-Q200 qualification for automotive applications.

faqs.question: How do I calculate breaking capacity for EV batteries?
faqs.answer: Battery fault current = Battery Voltage / (Battery Internal Resistance + Circuit Resistance). Large EV packs can deliver 20-50kA. Select fuse breaking capacity at least 25% above calculated fault current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate maximum fault current and add 25% margin for breaking capacity selection.

faqs.question: What mounting torque should I use?
faqs.answer: EV fuse bolt terminals typically require 25-30 Nm for M10 bolts and 12-15 Nm for M8 bolts. Use calibrated torque wrench and verify torque after thermal cycling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use specified torque values. Verify after installation and thermal cycling.

faqs.question: How do EV fuses coordinate with BMS?
faqs.answer: BMS handles moderate overcurrents via contactors. Fuses protect against high-current faults BMS cannot clear. Coordinate fuse time-current curve with BMS trip characteristics for layered protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Coordinate fuse with BMS protection curves. Fuse should clear faults BMS cannot handle.

products.name: 500A 1000V DC EV Battery Fuse
products.shortDescription: 500A 1000VDC fuse for EV battery pack protection with 50kA breaking capacity and AEC-Q200 automotive qualification.
descriptionParagraphs: The EV100-500-1 is a high-performance fuse designed for EV battery pack protection.
With 1000V DC rating and 500A current capacity, this fuse handles the demanding requirements of EV battery systems.
The 50kA breaking capacity ensures safe interruption of severe battery short-circuit faults.
features: Compact design for battery pack integration

faeReview.author: Steven Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - EV Systems
faeReview.content: EV battery protection requires careful attention to voltage, current, and breaking capacity. The EV100-500-1 meets all requirements for 800V EV battery systems. The 1000V rating provides margin for voltage transients, while 500A handles high-power applications. Breaking capacity of 50kA is adequate for most battery configurations, but always calculate the actual fault current to verify. The AEC-Q200 qualification ensures reliable operation in the harsh automotive environment with temperature extremes and vibration. Proper mounting torque (typically 25-30 Nm for M10) is critical for reliable electrical contact and thermal performance. I recommend this fuse for 800V battery pack main protection.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable protection for high-voltage EV battery systems

alternativeParts.reason: Lower current for smaller battery systems
alternativeParts.useCase: Lower power EVs or hybrid vehicles

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current for larger battery systems

companionParts.description: EV fuse holder for battery pack mounting

faqs.question: What torque should I use for mounting?
faqs.answer: M10 mounting bolts should be torqued to 25-30 Nm (18-22 ft-lb). Use calibrated torque wrench and tighten in star pattern if multiple bolts. Verify torque after initial installation and during maintenance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 25-30 Nm torque for M10 bolts.

faqs.question: Is this fuse suitable for 400V systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 1000V rating is suitable for 400V systems and provides excellent margin. However, for 400V systems, 500V or 600V rated fuses may be more economical. The 1000V rating is optimized for 800V systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Can be used for 400V, but consider lower voltage rated fuses for cost optimization.

faqs.question: What is the temperature derating for this fuse?
faqs.answer: The EV100-500-1 is rated for -40C to +125C ambient. Above 85C ambient, current derating may be required. Consult the datasheet for specific derating curves based on ambient temperature and cooling conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify operating temperature range. Apply derating for high ambient temperatures.

faqs.question: How do I verify AEC-Q200 qualification?
faqs.answer: AEC-Q200 qualification is documented in the fuse datasheet and certificate. Contact Bussmann or LiTong for qualification documentation including test reports and reliability data.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request AEC-Q200 documentation from your distributor.

faqs.question: What is the typical lifespan of this fuse?
faqs.answer: Under normal operating conditions, EV fuses last the lifetime of the vehicle. However, fuses should be inspected periodically and replaced if showing signs of heating, corrosion, or after any fault event.
faqs.decisionGuide: Inspect during regular maintenance. Replace after fault events or if showing degradation.

categories.name: Solar Fuses
categories.description: Bussmann solar fuses provide gPV-certified protection for photovoltaic systems including string protection, combiner boxes, and inverter input. These fuses are rated up to 1500VDC with gPV classification per IEC 60269-7.
categories.longDescription: Bussmann solar fuses are specifically designed for photovoltaic system protection with gPV certification per IEC 60269-7. As an authorized Bussmann distributor, LiTong provides fuses for string protection, combiner boxes, and inverter input applications. The PV series offers ratings up to 1500V DC with breaking capacities up to 50kA. Key features include UV-resistant construction for outdoor installations, compact sizes for combiner box integration, and gPV certification for safe DC interruption. Our selection guide helps you size fuses per NEC 690.9 requirements and select appropriate voltage ratings for your system design.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Solar Fuses
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting gPV fuses for photovoltaic applications based on string current and system voltage.

faqs.question: What is gPV certification?
faqs.answer: gPV (general Purpose Photovoltaic) per IEC 60269-7 is required for PV fuses. It indicates testing for DC interruption at PV voltages up to 1500V and ability to clear low overcurrents typical of PV faults. Standard fuses cannot safely interrupt PV circuits.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use gPV-certified fuses for PV applications.

faqs.question: How do I size solar string fuses?
faqs.answer: Per NEC 690.9, size fuses at 1.56 x module Isc (short-circuit current). Example: 10A Isc module needs minimum 15.6A fuse, use 15A or 20A standard rating. This accounts for increased current at low temperatures.
faqs.decisionGuide: Size at 1.56x string Isc. Round up to nearest standard rating.

faqs.question: What voltage rating do I need?
faqs.answer: Match fuse voltage to system maximum voltage including Voc at lowest temperature. For 1000V systems, use 1000V fuses. For 1500V systems, use 1500V fuses. Account for temperature effects on Voc.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate maximum system voltage and select fuse rating above this value.

faqs.question: Can I use standard fuses for solar applications?
faqs.answer: No. Standard AC fuses are not tested for DC interruption at PV voltages and may fail to clear faults safely. Always use gPV-certified fuses specifically designed for photovoltaic applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use gPV-certified fuses for solar. Never use standard AC fuses.

faqs.question: What is the difference between 1000V and 1500V fuses?
faqs.answer: 1500V fuses have enhanced insulation and creepage for higher voltages. 1500V systems allow longer strings, reducing cabling costs. Both must be gPV certified. Choose based on your system voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match fuse voltage to system design. Use 1500V for utility-scale systems.

products.name: 15A 1500V DC gPV Solar Fuse
products.shortDescription: 15A 1500VDC gPV fuse for string protection in solar combiner boxes with UV-resistant construction.
descriptionParagraphs: The PV-15A-1500 is a gPV-certified fuse designed for solar string protection.
With 1500V DC rating and 15A current capacity, this fuse meets the requirements of modern utility-scale solar installations.
The gPV certification ensures reliable DC interruption in photovoltaic applications.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Solar Systems
faeReview.content: The PV series is my go-to recommendation for solar combiner box protection. The 1500V rating supports the industry trend toward higher voltage systems that reduce DC cabling costs. The gPV certification is essential - I've seen standard fuses fail to clear PV faults, creating fire hazards. For string sizing, the fuse should be sized at 1.5-1.8x the string short-circuit current (Isc). For a typical string with 10A Isc, a 15A or 20A fuse is appropriate. The compact size fits standard PV fuse holders, making installation straightforward. Bussmann quality ensures reliable outdoor operation in all weather conditions. I recommend this fuse for any utility-scale solar installation.
faeReview.highlight: gPV-certified protection for utility-scale solar installations

alternativeParts.useCase: Strings with higher current modules (12-13A Isc)
Strings with lower current modules (6-7A Isc)

companionParts.description: 1500V rated PV fuse holder for combiner boxes

faqs.question: How do I size solar fuses for string protection?
faqs.answer: Solar fuse sizing: Fuse rating = 1.5-1.8 x string Isc. Use module Isc from datasheet (at STC). Consider temperature coefficient - Isc increases at low temperatures. Example: 10A Isc string needs 15A-18A fuse. Select next standard rating (15A or 20A).
faqs.decisionGuide: Size at 1.5-1.8x string Isc.

faqs.question: What size string can use this 15A fuse?
faqs.answer: The 15A fuse is suitable for strings with Isc of approximately 8-10A. Per NEC 690.9, 1.56 x 10A = 15.6A minimum. The 15A fuse is appropriate for typical 400-500W modules with Isc around 10A.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for strings with Isc 8-10A. Verify with module datasheet.

faqs.question: Is this fuse suitable for 1000V systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 1500V rating is suitable for 1000V systems and provides excellent margin. The higher voltage rating also allows for future system upgrades to 1500V without changing fuses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for 1000V systems with margin for future expansion.

faqs.question: What is the UV resistance rating?
faqs.answer: The PV fuse housing is made of UV-resistant materials suitable for permanent outdoor exposure. The fuse meets UL 248-19 requirements for photovoltaic applications including UV exposure testing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify UV resistance rating for outdoor installations.
